body.title: DS ToolLoop: per-ticket cost cap + DONE: prompt fix
body.result: cost cap  + DONE: prompt rewrite + cascade guard + temperature=0 explicit — 19 tests green
body.intention: I intend that DS completes S-size tickets at haiku tier without cascading to opus, and that cost overruns produce a diagnostic artifact rather than a silent money sink.
body.description:
OR models complete work correctly but return bare analysis text instead of DONE: prefix. DONE: gate fires, escalates to CC, Granny re-dispatches, full haiku→sonnet→opus cascade runs again. Burned ~$80 on 2026-06-11.

Two fixes:

1. PROMPT FIX — DONE: is the only valid non-tool-call response. Rewrite SYSTEM_PROMPT and SYSTEM_RULES so this is the first rule, not a footnote. Any prose response without DONE: or ESCALATE: is a model output bug.

2. COST CAP — replace the open-ended tier cascade with a per-ticket dollar ceiling. Cap = $3.00 (OR-rate equivalent of CC median ~$0.83 flat-rate; gives enough run to gather diagnostic data). Each turn accumulates running_cost from token counts at OR rates ($3/MTok input, $0.30/MTok cache_read, $15/MTok output). When running_cost > cap, ToolLoop exits with:

  COST_EXCEEDED: $X.XX of $3.00 cap — inference did not complete within cost constraint

This is NOT a budget failure and NOT an exception. It is a diagnostic signal: DS got to $X before completing. The escalation to CC carries the full turn log as a diagnostic artifact so the next attempt starts informed.

The cap IS the quality bar. If DS cannot close a ticket for what CC costs, escalate. No sense burning more to find out.

Cascade guard (orthogonal): do not advance haiku→sonnet when haiku executed tool calls but hit MAX_TURNS. Complexity signal, not a tier miss. Only advance tiers on zero-tool-call response.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py (SYSTEM_PROMPT, _run_inference), devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py (SYSTEM_RULES, ToolLoop.run)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** In: prompt rewrite enforcing DONE:, per-ticket cost accumulator with COST_EXCEEDED exit, cascade guard on tool-call count. Out: cross-ticket budget tracking, billing system integration, new OR models.
**Completion criteria:** (1) DS runs a trivial S-size ticket; haiku returns DONE: without cascading to sonnet/opus. (2) A ticket exceeding $3.00 exits with COST_EXCEEDED: $X of $3.00 cap (not an exception). (3) Escalation summary includes cost log as diagnostic artifact.
